violations.text: Empanadas were observed held at between 118-122 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated items and turned up heat. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026

The door to the outside, located in the front, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2026
Per interview with PIC, cat remains in basement. PIC was instructed to remove cat from establishment. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the prep unit have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026
The floors in the retail area is in poor repair (broken tiles). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as cheese) in the deli display unit. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated eggs. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

Cheese, lettuce, and salami were observed held between 45-60 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed items under refrigeration for active cooling. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

Deli meat and various cooked items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026
A cat was observed in basement area. PIC was instructed to remove cat from premises in previous inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The door to the outside, located in the front, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the prep unit and prep table have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026
The floors in the retail area is in poor repair (broken tiles). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2026
Inspector observed packaged cookies from an unapproved source. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

Various empanadas were observed held at between 93-120 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated and turned up hot-hold unit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

Cheese and milk were observed held at between 46-52 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed cheese and milk in walk-in cooler. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

Deli meat and cooked items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. PIC labeled. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The door to the outside, located in the front, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2026
A cat was observed in basement area. Owner was instructed to removed cat from premises. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the prep unit have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

Inspector observed a heavy accumulation of boxes outside around refuse area. Per interview w/ PIC, trash company has been delayed due to weather (snow). PIC stated trash removal is expected later today. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026
The floors in the retail area is in poor repair (broken tiles). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2026
